title: Show number of unattached tables when querying for a database info
description:
### Describe the improvement
Iceberg Catalog was created somewhere:
```
CREATE DATABASE catalog_demo
ENGINE = DataLakeCatalog
SETTINGS catalog_type = 'glue',
region = 'eu-west-1',
aws_access_key_id = 'key',
aws_secret_access_key = 'passwd';
USE catalog_demo;
SHOW TABLES;
┌─name─────────┐
1. │ foobar.test  │
2. │ foobar.test2 │
└──────────────┘
After that aws key was inactivated. Then after server restart is showed:
USE catalog_demo
SHOW TABLES
0 rows in set.
```
It is better to show a warning message that database wasn't attached successfully.
Also there is a possibility that Iceberg/DeltaLake tables exist in an ordinary database.
In this case it is better to show how many tables were not attached properly when executing "SHOW TABLES FROM" query.
### Which ClickHouse versions are affected?
Current release (25.7) and all versions before
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE catalog_demo.test (id INTEGER, name VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
SELECT COUNT(*) AS unattached_tables FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema = 'catalog_demo' AND table_name NOT IN (SELECT table_name FROM catalog_demo.test);
key_bug_features: Show unattached tables after database connection failure
